action: He wakes in the owner's suite and storms to the bridge, announcing himself captain to the empty helm.
thought: I'm still in charge, right?

action: He emerges from the frozen deck, strips off his outer layer without flinching, and heads inside to check the stove.
thought: Let him play captain. Ice doesn't care.

action: He wakes on his bunk in the stateroom, thinks quietly about the food supplies, and heads to the lounge to observe.
thought: Strategy requires patience and clear thinking.

action: He's already awake, mentally planning the day's priorities, and moves to the galley to organize supplies and take notes.
thought: Order prevents chaos. Chaos kills us.

action: He's out on the ice before sunrise, checking the fishing rig and gathering driftwood, soaked and grinning.
thought: Someone's gotta do the real work.

description: In the galley at dawn, all five gather around the stove and the food. Donald tries to assert command. Vladimir refuses it. Bibi proposes a system. Jinping observes. McGregor offers to work and leaves.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: Gentlemen, I've taken stock of the situation. As captain, I'll oversee all operations.

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: You have already given yourself a promotion. Congratulations.

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: Before we discuss hierarchy, we should inventory the yacht properly. I'll make a list.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: I've already inventoried. Food, water, fuel—I have a system.

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: Your system is breakfast in the captain's suite while others freeze. That is not leadership.

dialogue.speaker: xi
dialogue.line: Order must serve survival, not ego. What burns the stove today?

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: We ration carefully. Two wood-units per day to keep the fire steady.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Two units? I've already found driftwood on the floe. Fifteen more, maybe twenty if I haul it.

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: Then the problem is not fuel. It is discipline.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: Discipline starts with a clear captain. Everyone answers to me.

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: I will heat the stove. I will eat. The rest is your problem.

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: What if we establish a council? Each man responsible for one domain—navigation, supply, heat, security.

dialogue.speaker: xi
dialogue.line: And argue every day about borders? No. One voice gives orders. The question is whose.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Not his. He doesn't even know where the lifeboats are.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: I know exactly where the lifeboats are. I own this yacht.

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: The starboard lifeboat is sealed with ice. The port one is half-flooded. You will learn.

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: Gentlemen, panic is not survival. We can vote on a command structure when we've all—

dialogue.speaker: xi
dialogue.line: A yacht does not need five captains. It needs four workers and one coordinator.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: I volunteer to be one of the four. I'll catch fish, haul ice, keep us fed. You lot sort out rank.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: McGregor's right. I'll LEAD, and the rest work. That's how I built everything.

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: I have told you: I do not follow. I cooperate. There is a difference.

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: Let me propose: rotation. Each man leads for three days, then passes command to the next.

dialogue.speaker: xi
dialogue.line: Rotation works only if the men agree to follow. Do you agree, Vladimir?

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: I agree to stay alive. How I stay alive is my choice.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: That's the problem right there. Everyone's going to do whatever they want.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Yeah? Then we fail together. Or we work, and we don't. I'm fixing the fishing rig. Anyone want food, help or shut up.

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: Fair division of the catch?

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: The fish don't care about fairness. Neither do I.

dialogue.speaker: xi
dialogue.line: In a siege, the strongest commands. Are we in a siege?

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: We are always in a siege. The cold is the enemy. The man who fights it best will lead.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: I didn't get where I am by sitting on my hands—

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: You got where you are in a tower in New York. Here, you are on ice. Here, everything is new.

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: Fine. A trial: who can actually keep us alive? Let's reconvene at dinner. By then we'll see who brought food, water, heat.

dialogue.speaker: xi
dialogue.line: Agreed. Three days of work before we choose a captain.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: I don't need to earn it. I already AM captain.

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: Then prove it. Or stop saying it.

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: I think we're about to find out.

dialogue.speaker: xi
dialogue.line: A wise man knows when to lead and when to listen.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: Tremendous. Absolutely tremendous. I'm still in charge here...

action: Descends from the bridge with his written schedule, determined to establish meal protocols in the galley.
thought: I'm in control here. They'll see it.

action: Strips his heavy coat on the frozen deck, hauling split wood from the railings with methodical efficiency.
thought: I'll let him talk. The ice waits.

action: Sits quietly in the lounge reviewing the ship's manifest, noting who works and who merely talks.
thought: I observe. The cold will teach them.

action: Stands by the stove managing rations, balancing McGregor's exhaustion against Donald's protocols with practiced ease.
thought: If I keep them fed, maybe they'll listen.

action: Stumbles back to the yacht soaking wet and shaking, heading straight for the stove and hot food.
thought: I work. They talk. That's all that matters.

description: Midday in the galley: McGregor returns soaked from the ice demanding food while Donald insists on his schedule, and Bibi mediates with chowder and practicality.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: Everyone needs to hear the revised daily schedule. I've planned—

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: In a moment. McGregor's just come back. He's been working since dawn.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Working and freezing. Is there food, or are we rationing hope?

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: Of course there's food! I'm establishing a system for designated meals—

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Designated times? There's no time out there, just ice and nothing biting.

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: He's right, Donald. Let him eat something warm. Then we'll talk schedules.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: I'm not trying to starve him! I'm creating order! Without structure, chaos wins!

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: We're already in chaos. You're just adding talking to it.

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: Sit by the stove. I'll bring you something warm.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: Yes! But everyone sits in their assigned seats! I've numbered the stools—

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Jesus.

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: Donald, does it matter where we sit?

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: It matters for dignity! For respect! For—

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Vladimir's probably gathering fuel instead of listening to this mess.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: Exactly! He's supposed to FOLLOW my orders—wait, how do you know he's gathering fuel?

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Saw him on deck pulling wood off railings. Didn't ask permission from anyone.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: He NEEDS to ask permission! He's undermining—

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: He's helping us survive. We need that wood.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: I know we need wood! I DELEGATED the gathering to—

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: What is he in charge of, exactly?

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: Right now? Lunch.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: I'm not 'in charge of lunch.' I'm establishing procurement protocols—

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Boss, I'm hungry. Can protocol wait until I'm warm?

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: Fine. Yes. Eat.

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: Here. Fish chowder.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Where'd you get fish? I didn't catch anything.

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: Stores. There's more in the hold.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: See! System working! Food storage equals survival! Obviously planned—

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: This is actually good.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: It is? I mean, yes. It's excellent. Who made it?

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: You did. Three hours ago, before you went to bridge.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: I did? Well, obviously. Tremendous. Truly tremendous.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Can he hear himself?

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: No. It helps.

action: Paces the galley, gesturing emphatically, trying to impose order on the evening meal.
thought: My authority is finally taking root tonight.

action: Stands on the frozen open deck, shirtless in the arctic wind, watching the yacht lights flicker.
thought: Let him play captain. The ice doesn't care.

action: Sits in the lounge on a cushioned bench, nursing a cup of warm water, observing the evening quietly.
thought: Order emerges only when necessity demands it.

action: Ladles chowder in the galley, managing portions with meticulous care, the peacemaker's work never done.
thought: If they eat together, maybe they'll listen.

action: Enters the galley soaked in ice water, shaking himself dry, eyes fixed on the stove and the food.
thought: Six o'clock means nothing. Work never stops.

description: Evening in the galley around the stove. Donald tries to impose dinner protocol while McGregor, fresh from the ice, demands immediate food. Bibi mediates between them.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: We're establishing protocols. Dinner at six p.m., everyone at the table. Non-negotiable.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Protocols? I've been breaking ice for hours. I need food now, not a schedule.

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: Let's get McGregor warmed up first, Donald. He can listen while we eat.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: The point is order. Everyone gathers at six, every day. This is discipline.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Discipline? You're sitting here warm while I'm freezing out there actually working.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: Someone has to think strategically. Leadership means planning ahead.

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: He's right about that, McGregor. Routine does help people feel stable.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: I'm stable. I don't need his schedule for that.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: You need more than that. You need structure, purpose, the knowledge we're still civilized.

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: Is that what dinner at six is about, Donald?

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: It's about saying we haven't given up. That even in this cold, we maintain our humanity.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Is he serious right now?

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: He might be. And... he's not entirely wrong. Dinner at six does sound kind of nice.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Fine. I'll come at six. But I'm not dressing it up.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: That's all I ask. Tomorrow we establish watch schedules, sleeping arrangements, everything.

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: One thing at a time, Donald. Tonight, everyone needs rest.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Yeah. I need rest more than I need schedules.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: Rest is good. But tomorrow, things change. We become disciplined. Organized.

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: Tomorrow's fine. Tonight we have warmth, food, and each other. That's enough.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: That's all that matters. That's all we really need.

dialogue.speaker: trump
dialogue.line: Six p.m., everyone together. That's the start of real order.

dialogue.speaker: netanyahu
dialogue.line: Let's all sleep. Tomorrow comes fast in the cold.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: I'm with Bibi. Sleep sounds perfect right now.

action: Donald settled into the captain's chair on the bridge, savoring the moment of authority he'd seized at dinner.
thought: I proved it. My protocol took hold.

action: Vladimir moved silently through the galley in the dark, counting the fuel cans and assessing how long they had.
thought: Let him play captain. The ice decides.

action: Jinping lay in his bunk, watching the shadows on the cabin walls and thinking about what order really meant.
thought: The cold will teach them all something.

action: Bibi moved through the sleeping quarters, checking on people, worried that his carefully held mediation was already fracturing.
thought: It held for one day. How much longer?

action: McGregor came into the galley for warmth and found Vladimir doing the one thing nobody else was: thinking practically.
thought: Finally, someone who actually understands survival.

description: In the galley after dark, Vladimir checks the fuel while McGregor comes in from the cold, and between them the real power on the ship forms — quietly, practically, with no speeches.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Checking the stove, or just brooding in the dark?

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: Both. The fuel won't last five days at this rate.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Five days? Christ. And Donald's worried about dinner schedules.

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: He believes order creates safety.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Does it?

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: No. Work does. You understand this.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Someone has to. Bibi's trying, at least. But Jinping and Donald are useless.

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: They will learn when the cold teaches them.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: And what happens then?

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: We will see who remembers how to survive.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: You talk like you've done this before.

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: The cold respects those who respect it.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: That's almost poetic, mate.

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: It is practical. There is no poetry in freezing.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Fair. So what's your actual plan, then? Beyond counting fuel?

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: To stay alive. To make sure this ship stays alive. Everything else is secondary.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Finally, someone talking sense. I've been saying—

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: —that you are the only one working. Yes.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Christ, you were listening. Even when you look like you're not.

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: I listen to what matters. The west hold seal is failing. We should check it.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: Tonight? Now?

dialogue.speaker: putin
dialogue.line: The ice will not wait. Neither should we.

dialogue.speaker: conor
dialogue.line: You're right. Let me grab my coat.
